hawkish_phrases: The Committee remains highly attentive to inflation risks
additional policy firming would likely be warranted at future meetings
upside risks to the inflation outlook remained a key factor shaping the policy outlook
declines in inflation, particularly for measures of core inflation, had been slower than they had expected
dovish_phrases: Tighter credit conditions for households and businesses are likely to weigh on economic activity, hiring, and inflation
the extent to which additional increases in the target range may be appropriate after this meeting had become less certain
Several participants noted that if the economy evolved along the lines of their current outlooks, then further policy firming after this meeting may not be necessary
downside risks to growth and upside risks to unemployment had increased
the staff forecast prepared for the May FOMC meeting continued to assume...would lead to a mild recession starting later this year
summary: The Committee unanimously voted to raise the target range by 25 basis points to 5.00-5.25 percent but signaled heightened uncertainty about future rate increases, expressing that additional tightening 'may be appropriate' (softer language than prior meetings) while remaining highly attentive to inflation risks.
